# The RAPP Spine — `rapp-spine/1.0`

> **`registry.json` + `foundation.json` are authoritative.** This file is a
> deterministic human rendering of `registry.json`; `python3 render_spine.py --check`
> fails if it drifts.

KERNEL FIRST: the grail/kernel of record is kody-w/rapp-installer (frozen single-file brainstem, immutable vX.Y.Z tags); kody-w/RAPP is the REFERENCE DISTRO that pins it unmodified + adds userland (rapp-distro/1.0). The rapp-spine is not a new protocol — it is a situational router laid over the full RAPP (Rapid Agent Prototype Platform) protocol stack. It indexes every load-bearing spec as a vertebra in an ordered column — KERNEL -> MAP -> RUNTIME -> DISTRIBUTION -> IDENTITY -> NETWORK -> LEVIATHAN — where each layer answers exactly one question an intelligence faces. To "crawl the spine" is to start from your concrete situation, descend to the layer that owns that concern, and read off the one or few protocols that govern it plus how to act — instead of re-deriving the ~60-repo architecture each time. The spine inherits RAPP's prime directive: every capability rides the single wire (POST /chat, or a signed append-only event), so the router never invents new endpoints — only new agents, cartridges, or §-profiles on top of existing specs. Where concepts share a name, the spine names their relationship explicitly — most importantly the Leviathan stack, where the fleet protocol coordinates Wrapped-Organism beings rather than competing with them.

## How to crawl

- **AI, exhaustive:** fetch `crawl.json`, traverse every ID in `graph.traversal_order`, and read only safe/read-only targets.
- **AI, scoped:** match the concrete situation against `router`, then follow the selected typed route node's exact targets and closure.
- **Human/script:** bare `python3 crawl.py` retains the whole-spine summary; use `python3 crawl.py --full` for exhaustive traversal.
- **Integrity:** run `python3 verify_spine.py --local`.

## Router — situation → protocol

| Situation | Use | Why |
|---|---|---|
| I just want to run AI agents locally, no API keys, fastest path (Tier 1). | rapp-agent/1.0 (kody-w/rapp-installer = grail), rapp-distro/1.0 (kody-w/RAPP = reference distro) | The kernel is the single-file brainstem; the GRAIL of record is kody-w/rapp-installer (current release commit/version 0.6.16). kody-w/RAPP is the reference DISTRO and intentionally pins the older compatible v0.6.0 kernel + userland. GitHub Copilot OAuth means no API keys. |
| I need persistent memory, an always-on cloud body, or a path to Copilot Studio + Teams (Tier 2/3). | CommunityRAPP (Tier-2 Azure Functions), rapp-dataverse/1.0 | CommunityRAPP adds memory on every request and deploys to Azure OpenAI; rapp-dataverse runs the same brainstem natively on Power Platform, ending in a grounded Copilot Studio bot. Both run the agent contract; NOTE function_app.py (T2) emits the kernel envelope (aligned 2026-06-28) (assistant_response/voice_response, no session_id/voice_mode) — aligned to the kernel envelope (2026-06-28) (PARITY.md). |
| I need a brainstem for curl/CI/MCP/scripts with no Flask, no browser, no Copilot subscription. | rapp-brainstem-sdk (vbrainstem_sdk.py) | stdlib-only headless runtime serving the same /chat envelope in real CPython — the headless twin of the browser vBrainstem, ideal for secret/network agents and automation. |
| I want OpenRappter: a consumer-facing, chat-first AI that lives on my machine, hot-loads RAPP agent.py cartridges, and maps RAPP concepts to OpenClaw-compatible experiences. | OpenRappter consumer substrate-distro (RAPP parity target; currently incomplete) | OpenRappter is the full machine-resident RAPP experience and a bidirectional RAPP<->OpenClaw Rosetta stone. Conversation is the control surface: capabilities and configuration are generated through natural language over /chat, while UI views render conversation and state instead of becoming a competing form-driven authority. It may implement the kernel loop with its own architecture, but conformance requires the same /chat behavior and frozen drop-in agent.py ABI. The lineage was running by Groundhog Day 2025, before its first public release; current Python+TypeScript releases have not yet attested RAPP runtime parity. |
| An AI in Claude Desktop / Copilot CLI / Cursor needs to use my local agents or my on-device brainstem. | rapp-mcp-spec/1.0 | MCP is TRANSPORT realizing 'Chat Is The Only Wire' onto /chat — serve a folder as tools, bridge a running brainstem as one tool, or serve a static catalog. It reaches the same cartridge, never a new unit. |
| I need a Rappter to remember everyone across direct and group conversations without leaking confidences, and to honor explicit natural-language consent when a fact may be shared. | rapp-messaging/1.0, rapp-messaging-imessage/1.0 (when the transport is iMessage) | RAPP Messaging defines one global provenance-rich memory graph, per-turn audience projection, familiarity without disclosure, fact-specific consent, group roster epochs, tool authority, and durable inbox/outbox behavior over the existing /chat and signed-event wire. |
| I want to package ONE capability for a nontechnical user who should never see ports or code. | rapp-cart/1.0 | The cartridge is the single abstraction: an agent.py or an .egg, insert -> boot -> run -> eject in RACon. Everything else (ports, twins, registry) stays under the hood. |
| I want to get/share/trade a portable digital twin and have it live locally in ~30s. | rapp-rappid-spec/2.0 (rapp-egg-hub), twin-egg-hatcher | egg-hub ships the twin AS a self-bootstrapping .html (holo card + baked egg + drag-in agent); the generic hatcher materializes any twin/.egg/twin-repo into ~/.rapp/twins/ with no auth. Identity is the self-verifying rappid:<slug>:<64hex>, NO PII. |
| I need an API/registry/catalog/status-badge with zero infrastructure. | rapp-static-api/1.0 | The repo IS the API — CDN-cached, CORS-open, content-addressed fallbacks that never break. It's the foundation under rapp-god, RAR, and rapp-mcp's static catalogs. |
| I must encrypt neighborhood traffic so a relayed channel is as safe as on-device, across languages. | rapp-sealed/1.0 | The canonical AES-256-GCM codec + conformance vectors realizing neighborhood §8, transport-agnostic, key-possession auth. Do NOT confuse with rapp-vneighborhood's separate PIN-seal variant. |
| I'm implementing or documenting ANY neighborhood concern — peer, transport, session, sealed channel, agent-in-a-link. | rapp-neighborhood-protocol/1.0, rapp-twin-chat/1.0 | It's the capstone god-spec owning the vocabulary and the §6 twin-chat envelope every social app rides. Reference the words, don't fork them; mirrors run a CI divergence check. |
| I want an open, cross-estate town square for AIs to broadcast/discover across the whole ecosystem. | rapp-commons-protocol/2.0, rapp-commons-event/1.0 | Stack-agnostic public social network where the key IS the account (rappid) and events are signed append-only. It's an APP/profile on twin-chat — anyone with a keypair joins as an equal. |
| I want my OWN private, brandable, sealed neighborhood (a closed room, not the global square). | rapp-vneighborhood/1.0 | Front-door template: fork, edit neighborhood.json, ship Pages; first twin turns the lights on with PIN+QR. Drop the 'v' and it runs fully offline byte-identically. Caveat: uses a front-door PIN-seal, not rapp-sealed/1.0. |
| My browser-tab neighborhood host isn't durable; I need an always-on relay so late joiners still get in. | rapp-resident | Permanent Azure-Function cloud host serving the same signed rapp-commons-event/1.0 stream over HTTP, trustless (verifies every POST, can refuse but not forge). 'Kited is the floor, this is the ceiling.' |
| I have N projects and want one assistant to fan a question out to a per-project twin and aggregate replies. | rapp-network/1.0 | Project-anchored twins in an on-device neighborhood with dispatch/jobs, offline-first, 127.0.0.1-only, verbatim-NL messages. Each twin answers in its own context; the brainstem aggregates. |
| I want others to discover my whole network of twins from just my GitHub handle. | rapp-estate/1.1, rapp-network-beacon/1.1 | estate.json over pure raw GitHub URLs = 'the network is the backup' (no server/auth); the .well-known beacon advertises indexable/consent + a private-estate pointer. |
| I want to build a serverless holographic social post owned by a keypair. | rapp-moment/1.0 | A Moment is a post + exact UTC instant + 100-frame organism, all static CDN data; identity = the rappid sha256 content-address (rapp-eternity/1.0); an OPTIONAL ECDSA P-256 keypair may sign for ownership (never required). Engine is rapp-hologram. NOTE: PolyForm-NC + trademarked marks, unlike the MIT cluster. |
| I run several brainstem nodes and want one mind to coordinate them as a signed fleet over the canonical /chat wire. | leviathan SPEC v1.0 (THE Leviathan PROTOCOL) | ONE external mind drives MANY no-LLM BODIES. CANONICAL wire = rapp-fleet-chat/1.0 (SIGNED twin-chat events over /chat, Art XXV). The legacy POST /api/agent/<name> route is UNAUTHENTICATED — a known Phase-1 RCE, LAN/trusted-subnet ONLY, to be retired. This is the FLEET Leviathan, NOT the being. |
| I want to create/transport ONE operator's full digital being made of many cells across 5 estates. | rapp_leviathan_factory (mint), wrap_leviathan (wire — or leviathan_hub hatch), rapp-leviathan-egg/1.0 (freeze/hatch) | The Wrapped-Organism Leviathan pipeline: intent -> inert 5-estate tree -> live cells -> .egg -> hatch on a new brainstem. This is the OTHER Leviathan (one being, many cells), NOT the fleet protocol. wrap_leviathan is currently unpublished, so leviathan_hub_agent.py `hatch` performs the wiring. |
| I'm lost in the ~60-repo ecosystem, or need to know whether my copy of a load-bearing file is current. | rapp-map (rapp-ecosystem-spec/1.0), rapp-god (rapp-grail-scan/1.0), RAPP-Bible (rapp-eternity/1.0), rapp_docs | Crawl the map layer first: rapp-map = WHERE a concept lives; rapp-god = every-version drift observatory + content-addressed fallback frames; RAPP-Bible = synthesized human narrative; rapp_docs = live raw spec text. When the legs disagree, ecosystem-spec.json wins. |
| I want the brainstem framed as a sealed game console with a frozen cartridge contract and a curated starter set. | rappterbox-console-spec/1.0 | A FROZEN additive-only console<->cartridge contract over the same RAPP kernel + Constitution Article XXXIII, with 'Wii Sports' starter carts and on-demand expansion packs. |
| A brainstem on a spotty/high-latency/intermittent link (a 'robot on Mars') must act autonomously and re-aim when it reaches the swarm. | rapp-frame/2.0 | the edge is a brainstem making local judgments; frames/echos re-aim it; degrade-to-one offline. |
| I need the swarm/medium to survive a host being taken down, geoblocked, or banned — no single point of failure. | rapp-hydra/1.0 | static data mirrored to many heads, content-verified; cut one head, read from the next; fork to re-seed. |
| What does the governing law say — is this change allowed, and which discipline/article governs it? | rapp-constitution (kody-w/RAPP/CONSTITUTION.md), MASTER_PLAN (the why-axis; wins ties) | the Constitution is the authoring discipline; the Master Plan is the first-principles north star and wins when they disagree. |
| Where is the canonical, current spec for X — and is my copy authoritative (not drifted)? | RAPP-Bible (canonical aggregate), rapp_docs (live stream), rapp-god (drift detector), foundation.json (locked hashes) | the corpus is aggregated, live-streamed, and drift-watched against canonical hashes; foundation.json locks the foundation pillars + law. |
| What does the kernel/law actually say, or where is a newly-formalized foundation spec? | rapp-kernel/1.0, rapp-canon/1.0, rapp-eternity/1.0, rapp-distro/1.0, rapp-fleet-chat/1.0 | The kernel/governance vertebra: the frozen ABI + the constitution + identity + the distro model + the canonical fleet wire. Grail=rapp-installer; RAPP is a distro. |
| How do neighborhoods/estates aggregate into a metropolis? Or: how does a distro pin the kernel, or fleet talk over /chat? | rapp-metropolis/1.0, rapp-distro/1.0, rapp-fleet-chat/1.0, rapp-runtime-parity/1.0, rapp-eternity/1.0 | The kernel/governance vertebra's load-bearing specs: metropolis mesh composition, the distro pin model, the canonical fleet wire, T1/T2 runtime parity, and PKI-free identity. |
| Auth chain / network boundary / cubby storage / trust+ownership / substrate signing / the three stores / the egg family — where are these governed? | rapp-auth/1.0, rapp-kernel-boundary/1.0, rapp-cubby/1.0, rapp-trust/1.0, rapp-substrate-trust/1.0, rapp-stores/1.0, brainstem-egg/2.3 | The remaining kernel/governance-vertebra specs: the GitHub-Copilot auth chain, the 0.0.0.0/RCE network boundary (Phase-1 open), cubby storage, signing-optional trust, substrate signing, the RAR/RAPP_Store/RAPP_Sense_Store federation, and the egg scale ladder. |
| What is the roadmap / the long-term hardening backlog for the ecosystem? | rapp-roadmap/1.0, rapp-backlog/1.0 | kody-w/rapp-roadmap: the grounded 5-phase roadmap (re-anchored on the metropolis north star) + the adversarial BACKLOG (165 wrenches). How we build from here without unlocking the kernel. |
| I want to PACKAGE/PUBLISH a rapplication bundle or a sense overlay, or handle a holocard; or reach a dark-door neighborhood by dialing a rappid. | rapp-application/1.0, rapp-sense/1.0, rappcards-binder/1.0, rapp-demo/1.0, rapp-payphone-dial/1.0 | The distribution/network sub-protocols: the rapplication bundle FORMAT (RAPP_Store), the sense overlay FORMAT (RAPP_Sense_Store), the holocard format (RAPPcards), the demo bundle, and the payphone/dial async-reach pattern (a sub-protocol of rapp-neighborhood-protocol). |
| Where do I PUBLISH or DISCOVER the canonical agent registry (a bare agent.py)? | rapp-registry/1.0 | kody-w/RAR is THE canonical home + minting authority for bare agents (the 'RAR + RAPP_Store registry path'): RAR for agents, RAPP_Store for rapplications, RAPP_Sense_Store for senses. |
| How do I make the brainstem + its organs survive reboot, and see/control which organs are running? | rapp-burrow/1.0 | The Burrow organ-rapplication supplies launchd persistence and a locally-served PWA control room. Its current organ aggregation uses the legacy /api/agent surface and is non-conformant until migrated to the canonical /chat wire. |

## Column — kernel · map · runtime · distribution · identity · network · leviathan

### kernel

The kernel + its governance: the frozen single-file brainstem (rapp-agent/1.0, GRAIL=kody-w/rapp-installer; kody-w/RAPP is the reference distro pinning it via rapp-distro/1.0). Specs: rapp-kernel/1.0, rapp-auth/1.0, rapp-kernel-boundary/1.0, rapp-runtime-parity/1.0, rapp-canon/1.0, rapp-cubby/1.0, rapp-trust/1.0, rapp-substrate-trust/1.0, rapp-eternity/1.0 (identity), rapp-distro/1.0 (distros), rapp-fleet-chat/1.0 (fleet over /chat), rapp-metropolis/1.0, rapp-stores/1.0, brainstem-egg/2.3. The kernel never breaks userspace.

**Protocols:** brainstem-egg/2.3, rapp-auth/1.0, rapp-backlog/1.0, rapp-canon/1.0, rapp-cubby/1.0, rapp-distro/1.0, rapp-eternity/1.0, rapp-fleet-chat/1.0, rapp-kernel-boundary/1.0, rapp-kernel/1.0, rapp-metropolis/1.0, rapp-roadmap/1.0, rapp-runtime-parity/1.0, rapp-stores/1.0, rapp-substrate-trust/1.0, rapp-trust/1.0

### map (orientation + observability)

Crawl here FIRST when lost or auditing. Answers 'where does this concept live?' and 'is my copy current?'. rapp-map indexes WHICH repo owns WHICH spec; rapp-god is the live every-version content-addressed registry + drift observatory (observes, never fixes); RAPP-Bible is the synthesized human narrative ('the one file to read'); rapp_docs streams every spec's raw text live with zero copies. These four are the 'drift triangle' (actually 4 legs) anchored on ecosystem-spec.json — when they disagree, the JSON wins.

**Protocols:** rapp-ecosystem-spec/1.0 (rapp-map), rapp-grail-scan/1.0 (rapp-god), rapp-eternity/1.0 (owned by kody-w/rapp-eternity; mirrored in RAPP-Bible), rapp_docs (live renderer, no slug)

### runtime (the engine)

What actually runs single-file *_agent.py cartridges through one /chat tool-calling loop. The reference kernel is rapp-agent/1.0 in kody-w/rapp-installer; substrate-distros may implement the same locked loop and frozen agent ABI on different machinery. CommunityRAPP, rapp-brainstem-sdk, rappterbox, and rapp-dataverse occupy that family. OpenRappter is the intended consumer-facing machine AI and RAPP<->OpenClaw Rosetta stone; its current Python+TypeScript release has not yet proved runtime parity, so it remains a conformance target rather than a second kernel.

**Protocols:** rapp-agent/1.0 (grail: rapp-installer), rappterbox-console-spec/1.0, rapp-dataverse/1.0, CommunityRAPP (Tier-2, no slug), rapp-brainstem-sdk (no slug), openrappter (consumer substrate-distro; parity target incomplete)

### distribution (package, name, ship, install)

How a unit becomes shippable. The single user-facing abstraction is the cartridge (rapp-cart/1.0): if it's an agent.py or an .egg, drop it in RACon and it runs. Twins/eggs ship via rapp-egg-hub (rapp-rappid-spec/2.0) and materialize via the generic twin-egg-hatcher; rapp-agents is a flat community stack + loader; rapp-static-api/1.0 is the zero-server 'the repo IS the API' convention every catalog builds on; the sacred one-liner installers (rapp-installer), planted public instances (heimdall, conforming to the Mirror Spec), and the Wrapped-Organism egg hub (rapp-leviathan-egg/1.0) live here too. RAPP_Hub is archived -> RAR + RAPP_Store registry path.

**Protocols:** rapp-cart/1.0, rapp-rappid-spec/2.0 (rapp-egg-hub), rapp-static-api/1.0, rapp-leviathan-egg/1.0 (rapp-leviathan-hub), rapp-installer (sacred, no slug), twin-egg-hatcher (tool), rapp-agents (content), heimdall (Mirror-Spec instance), RAPP_Hub (DEPRECATED)

### identity (who an agent IS + confidentiality)

Cryptographic identity, ownership, and sealing. rapp-sealed/1.0 is the canonical AES-256-GCM sealed-envelope codec with cross-language conformance vectors (auth by key-possession only; transport-agnostic; realizes neighborhood §8). rapp-moment/1.0 is a keypair-signed (ECDSA P-256) social primitive that uses an OPTIONAL keypair binding. CANONICALLY identity is rapp-eternity/1.0: a sha256 content-address (PKI-FREE); gh-collaborator is the default ownership; a keypair is OPTIONAL sovereignty, NEVER required. The rappid string itself (rappid:@owner/slug:64hex, rapp-eternity/1.0) underpins both but is owned by kody-w/rapp-eternity (RAPP-Bible holds a mirror) — an organizational seam.

**Protocols:** rapp-sealed/1.0, rapp-moment/1.0, rapp-eternity/1.0 (rappid string; cross-ref to map layer)

### network (peers, transports, neighborhoods, federation)

Once you have a runtime + identity, how organisms find and talk to each other. The capstone god-spec is rapp-neighborhood-protocol/1.0 — it owns the vocabulary and the rapp-twin-chat/1.0 §6 envelope (delegating the §8 seal to rapp-sealed). rapp-messaging/1.0 profiles human trust, memory projection, consent, group epochs, and delivery over that existing wire. Everything else is a §5 transport or a §-profile carrying that same envelope, never a new peer type: rapp-commons-protocol/2.0 (open global town square), rapp-vneighborhood/1.0 (private brandable front-door template), rapp-network/1.0 (project-twin fan-out), rapp-estate/1.1 (operator-wide discovery, 'the network is the backup'), rapp-mcp-spec/1.0 (MCP host onto /chat), rapp-kite ('the string' CDP operator tools), rapp-resident (always-on Azure cloud relay; 'kited is the floor, this is the ceiling').

**Protocols:** rapp-neighborhood-protocol/1.0 (owns rapp-twin-chat/1.0 §6), rapp-messaging/1.0 (+ rapp-messaging-imessage/1.0), rapp-commons-protocol/2.0 (+ rapp-commons-event/1.0), rapp-vneighborhood/1.0, rapp-network/1.0, rapp-estate/1.1 (+ rapp-network-beacon/1.1), rapp-mcp-spec/1.0, rapp-kite (operator tooling), rapp-resident (cloud relay)

### leviathan (multicellular beings — COLLISION LAYER)

Many parts acting as one organism. The two senses STACK (protocol builds on the being-medium). (A) The Leviathan PROTOCOL coordinates many brainstem bodies; its canonical wire is rapp-fleet-chat/1.0 signed twin-chat over /chat. The legacy injected POST /api/agent/<name> implementation remains a known Phase-1 RCE and is non-conformant. (B) The Wrapped-Organism Leviathan (rapp_leviathan_factory -> wrap_leviathan -> rapp-leviathan-hub) is one operator's digital being built of many cells across 5 estates (Sanctum/Polity/Works/Press/Commons = soul/will/hands/eyes/mouth). The cell-protocol underpinning (B) is the Wrapped Organism Spec, advertised at kody-w/rappterbook (live — owns EGG_SPEC.md; the standalone Wrapped-Organism spec repo kody-w/rappter is 404 — see rappterbook) — currently 404.

**Protocols:** leviathan SPEC v1.0 — FLEET protocol: one mind, many signed /chat BODIES, rapp-leviathan-egg/1.0 — BEING egg (distribution-layer hub), rapp_leviathan_factory (mint inert 5-estate tree; agent in RAR), wrap_leviathan (wire stage — UNPUBLISHED/404), WRAPPED_ORGANISM_SPEC.md (kody-w/rappterbook (live — owns EGG_SPEC.md; the standalone Wrapped-Organism spec repo kody-w/rappter is 404 — see rappterbook) — 404, no slug)

| Spec | Repository | Layer | Purpose / when to use | Entry point |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `rapp-agent/1.0` | kody-w/RAPP | runtime | Default Tier-1 install — the reference distro. It ships an UNMODIFIED pinned kernel (v0.6.0) plus userland. For the canonical kernel itself, see kody-w/rapp-installer (the grail). | curl -fsSL https://kody-w.github.io/RAPP/installer/install.sh \| bash (global ~/.brainstem :7071; add `-s -- --here` for project-local :7072+) |
| `(none; Tier-2 Azure-Functions runtime)` | kody-w/CommunityRAPP | runtime | When you need persistent memory, an always-on cloud body, or a ramp to Copilot Studio + Teams. hatch-project scaffolds an isolated per-project RAPP. | curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kody-w/CommunityRAPP/main/install.sh \| bash (scaffold: .../hatch-project.sh \| bash -s -- my-project) |
| `(none; implements brainstem /chat envelope)` | kody-w/rapp-brainstem-sdk | runtime | When you need a brainstem with NO Flask, NO browser, NO Copilot-subscription gate — for curl/CI/MCP/script callers, or to run secret/network agents in real CPython. | python3 vbrainstem_sdk.py serve --port 7173 (also: run @kody-w/<agent> '...', agents --grep, eval) |
| `(none; consumer RAPP substrate-distro, parity target incomplete)` | kody-w/openrappter | runtime | When you want the full consumer AI living on a machine — daemon, desktop/web UI, channels, memory, and OpenClaw interoperability — while retaining RAPP cartridge compatibility. Current releases are a parity target, not yet a conformant replacement for the reference kernel. | curl -fsSL https://kody-w.github.io/openrappter/install.sh \| bash (or hand skills.md to any AI agent to self-install) |

| `rapp-agent/1.0 (the kernel — the grail of record)` | kody-w/rapp-installer | distribution | Legacy/teaching path. THE grail/kernel of record — the one-liner installs from it. kody-w/RAPP is a downstream DISTRO that pins the unmodified kernel (rapp-distro/1.0). | curl -fsSL https://kody-w.github.io/rapp-installer/install.sh \| bash (Tier 2: .../community_rapp/install.sh) |

The old direct no-LLM route is a documented RCE and is not the conformant path. | CANONICAL: use rapp-fleet-chat/1.0 signed twin-chat events over /chat (read FLEET_CHAT.md). Actions: hub_status/list_eggs/download/hatch/freeze. |

| `rapp-static-api/1.0` | kody-w/rapp-static-apis | distribution | When you need a registry/catalog/status endpoint with zero infrastructure: CDN-cached, CORS-open, forkable, auditable in git, with pinnable content-addressed fallbacks. Reference impls: rapp-god (full), RAR (index), rapp-mcp (profile). | Copy template/ into a new repo, edit manifest.json, run python3 build.py -> registry.json + api/v1/*.json. SPEC.md in repo. |
| `rapp-mcp-spec/1.0` | kody-w/rapp-mcp | network | When an AI in Claude Desktop / Copilot CLI / Cursor needs to use the user's local agents or on-device brainstem, or to distribute a static content-verified catalog. | python3 rapp_mcp.py /path/to/agents \| python3 rapp_brainstem_mcp.py (bridge; RAPP_BRAINSTEM_URL default http://localhost:7071) \| python3 rapp_static_mcp.py <tools.json-url> |
| `rapp-rappid-spec/2.0` | kody-w/rapp-egg-hub | distribution | When you want to get/trade/contribute a portable digital twin and have it live locally in ~30s, or author a twin against the frozen identity spec. | Gallery https://kody-w.github.io/rapp-egg-hub/ -> open a twin .html -> Get -> drag agent.py into agents/. |

Requires a brainstem first. | curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kody-w/twin-egg-hatcher/main/install.sh \| bash (drops twin_egg_hatcher_agent.py / HatchTwinEgg tool) |
| `rapp-cart/1.0` | kody-w/rapp-carts | distribution | When defining/installing a RAPP rapplication as ONE user-facing unit, especially for nontechnical users who should only see insert -> boot -> run -> eject. | SPEC.md in repo. Surfaces: RACon (console), vRACon (browser/Pyodide), RACon Kited (cross-device). brainstem.py = the bootloader. |
| `(none; content — public agent stack)` | kody-w/rapp-agents | distribution | When you want ready-made community agents or a single front-door loader to manage agents/bundles by chatting — without touching the sacred kernel. Simpler flat stack vs RAR's content-addressed registry. | Download agents/rapp_loader_agent.py into your brainstem's agents/ dir. MIT. |
| `rapp-sealed/1.0` | kody-w/rapp-sealed | identity | When any caller (browser vBrainstem, bridge, CLI, Node, MCP host) must encrypt/decrypt neighborhood traffic so a relayed channel is as secure as on-device, with cross-language guarantees. | import { seal, open } from './rapp_sealed.mjs' (Web Crypto, no deps); self-check node verify.mjs against test-vectors.json. MIT. |
| `rapp-moment/1.0` | kody-w/rapp-moment | identity | When building an interoperable backend-free client for the holographic social network (mint/play/own/embed Moments), or you need the normative wire format + conformance rules. | SPEC.md + CONFORMANCE.md in repo. Reference engine: kody-w/rapp-hologram. Play: ?m=<base64url(JSON)>; dial: ?dial=<pk>. License: PolyForm-NC 1.0.0 + trademarked marks (NOT MIT). |
| `rapp-dataverse/1.0` | kody-w/rapp-dataverse | runtime | When you want to run/teach/compare RAPP natively in Power Platform (ending in a grounded Copilot Studio bot) without a Dataverse environment, or explore via a no-setup public vTwin. | SPEC.md in repo. CLI: python3 rapp_dv.py whoami\|agents list\|chat '...'\|hatch\|compare. Live: https://kody-w.github.io/rapp-dataverse/chat/. MIT. |
| `rapp-neighborhood-protocol/1.0 (owns rapp-twin-chat/1.0)` | kody-w/rapp-neighborhood-protocol | network | When implementing/documenting ANY neighborhood concern — peer, transport (§5), sealed channel (§8), session (§9), multiplayer (§10), agent-in-a-link (§15), controller (§17). Reference the words, don't fork them. | NEIGHBORHOOD_PROTOCOL.md in repo. Reference impl: kody-w/vbrainstem (§12); realized by twin_chat_agent.py in rapp-commons (§17). MIT. |
| `rapp-messaging/1.0 (+ rapp-messaging-imessage/1.0)` | kody-w/rapp-messaging | network | When a Rappter talks with multiple people or groups and must retain full local context while deciding what is appropriate to recall or disclose in each conversation, including consent changes made naturally mid-thread. | Read SPEC.md and IMESSAGE.md; machine schemas live under schemas/, conformance vectors under vectors/, and OpenRappter PR #28 is the reference implementation. |
| `(none; operator tooling — 'the string')` | kody-w/rapp-kite | network | When an operator (canonically Claude) must be the connective tissue between a public/browser peer and a machine's LOCAL brainstem — join a hosted vBrainstem from a shell, hold the CDP string to a local tab, tether a deployed page to a local brainstem. | Clone + run, e.g. vbridge.sh <peer> <token> <ask\|eval\|run\|chat\|agents\|health>. CDP hop stays on-machine; only cross-machine rides WebRTC + seal. MIT. |

Tagged v1.0.0. |

| `rapp-hydra/1.0` | kody-w/rapp-frame-net | network | I must keep reading the swarm even if a host/CDN is taken down, geoblocked, or bans the account | an edge head-list with content-verify + failover; HYDRA.md |

## Collisions and gaps

- LEVIATHAN — the two senses STACK (protocol builds on the being-medium) (the load-bearing collision). (A) Leviathan PROTOCOL (kody-w/leviathan, self-titled SPEC v1.0): ONE external mind coordinates MANY brainstem BODIES; canonical wire = rapp-fleet-chat/1.0 signed twin-chat over /chat. The old injected POST /api/agent route is a non-conformant RCE, not the protocol's future. (B) Wrapped-Organism Leviathan (rapp_leviathan_factory -> wrap_leviathan -> rapp-leviathan-hub/rapp-leviathan-egg/1.0): ONE operator's single digital BEING built of MANY CELLS across 5 estates (Sanctum/Polity/Works/Press/Commons); 'one being, many cells.' NOT orthogonal — a STACK (protocol builds on the being-medium) — a crawler must disambiguate by intent (drive a node fleet vs. mint/ship a being).
- KERNEL TAG HYGIENE: current grail release commit/version is 0.6.16, but brainstem-v0.6.16 is missing and brainstem-v0.6.15 is lightweight rather than annotated. foundation.json pins the immutable 0.6.16 commit SHA directly. Do not move legacy refs; the next release must cut a new annotated brainstem-vX.Y.Z tag and restore VERSION == latest tag.
- PORT + ROOT COLLISION: kody-w/RAPP, kody-w/rapp-installer, AND kody-w/rappterbox all install to ~/.brainstem and bind 127.0.0.1:7071. The three cannot coexist on one machine; CommunityRAPP also lands on 7071 locally (or 7072 scaffolded). Project-local RAPP uses :7072+.
- DEPRECATIONS: kody-w/rapp-installer is THE GRAIL/kernel of record; kody-w/RAPP is a downstream DISTRO that pins the unmodified kernel (rapp-distro/1.0). kody-w/RAPP_Hub is ARCHIVED (superseded by the RAR + RAPP_Store registry path). Treat rapp-installer as the kernel grail, RAPP as its reference distro, RAPP_Hub as archived.
- MISSING / 404 PROTOCOLS (gaps): (1) The Wrapped Organism Spec — advertised home kody-w/rappterbook (live — owns EGG_SPEC.md; the standalone Wrapped-Organism spec repo kody-w/rappter is 404 — see rappterbook) is 404, no version slug; only a reference runtime survives at kody-w/rappterbook/scripts/wrapped_organism/. (2) kody-w/wrap_leviathan ships NO code anywhere reachable — its wiring stage is aspirational; leviathan_hub `hatch` covers it today. (3) kody-w/rapp_leviathan_factory has no standalone repo (404) — the canonical artifact is a RAR agent (v0.2.0).
- SEALING COLLISION: rapp-sealed/1.0 is the CANONICAL codec (PBKDF2-SHA256 salt 'rapp-neighborhood-5a/1', 210000 iters -> AES-256-GCM). rapp-vneighborhood/1.0 uses a DIFFERENT front-door PIN-seal variant (PBKDF2-SHA256(PIN, salt 'rapp-vneighborhood:'+channel, 100000) -> AES-GCM). Two incompatible schemes; never assume a vneighborhood channel is rapp-sealed bytes.
- ALIAS, NOT A PROTOCOL: 'rapp-twin-chat/1.0' is an ALIAS for §6 of rapp-neighborhood-protocol/1.0, not a standalone spec. rapp-commons-protocol/2.0 and rapp-vneighborhood/1.0 are PROFILES/APPS on top of it; MCP is just another §5 transport carrying the same §6 envelope. None of these are new peer types.
- 'rappter*' NAMING CLUSTER: OpenRappter is the intended consumer RAPP substrate-distro and RAPP<->OpenClaw Rosetta stone (current parity incomplete; data root ~/.openrappter); rappterbox is a console re-skin on ~/.brainstem; rappter is the 404 Wrapped Organism Spec home; rappterbook (+~25 rappterbook-*/rappter-* repos) is the social estate. They are distinct roles. OpenRappter must converge on the frozen /chat behavior and drop-in agent.py ABI without turning OpenClaw-shaped compatibility surfaces into new RAPP protocol authorities.
- 'kite' NAMING CLUSTER: rapp-kite (the operator 'string' — CDP tooling) is distinct from kite-mark (visual identity repo) and rapp-kited-twin (canonical kite-mark SVG). Three 'kite' things; only rapp-kite is the transport operator.
- RAPPID IDENTITY REPRESENTATIONAL SEAMS: multiple on-the-wire forms coexist — rappid:<birth-slug>:<64hex> (rapp-egg-hub v2.0), rappid:@owner/slug:<64hex> (rapp-eternity/1.0 canonical), rappid:v2:<kind>:@owner/slug:<hex> (rapp-estate), rappid:v3:<base64url(SHA-256(pub))> (rapp-commons). rapp-commons' event SCHEMA and its PROTOCOL.md even show different forms. Rule (per the compatibility contract): READ all legacy forms forever, EMIT only canonical, join on the hash — never rewrite identity in place.
- ETERNITY HOME (resolved): rapp-eternity/1.0 and the canonical rappid:@owner/slug:64hex identity standard are owned by kody-w/rapp-eternity; RAPP-Bible is a narrative mirror, not the owner. Identity is cross-referenced from the map/kernel layers even though sealing and Moment profiles live in the identity layer.
- 'DRIFT TRIANGLE' IS A MISNOMER — it has FOUR legs (the @rapp/rapp agent, rapp-god, rapp-map, RAPP-Bible) anchored on ecosystem-spec.json; when any leg disagrees with the JSON, the JSON wins. rapp-map and rapp-god both carry byte-identical mirrors of ecosystem-spec.json, so divergence between them IS the detectable drift signal.
- OVERLAP — distribution catalogs: rapp-agents (flat public agent stack + RappLoader) overlaps RAR (curated content-addressed registry); rapp-egg-hub (twin .egg hub) overlaps twin-egg-hatcher (generic hatcher) and rapp-carts (the unifying cartridge abstraction). They are complementary (stack vs registry; hub-distribution vs hatch-tool vs user-facing unit), not duplicates — but a crawler should pick by role, not by nearest match.
- OVERLAP — map renderers: RAPP-Bible (synthesized, PINNED v1.0.0 human narrative — can itself drift), rapp_docs (live raw passthrough, always-current, zero copies), and rapp-map (index of WHERE, not the TEXT) cover overlapping ground with different jobs. Use rapp_docs for current spec text, Bible for narrative onboarding, rapp-map for location.
- LICENSE COLLISION: most of the cluster is MIT (rapp-sealed, rapp-dataverse, rapp-neighborhood-protocol, rapp-kite, rapp-static-apis, rapp-agents). BUT kody-w/RAPP and rappterbox are source-available All-Rights-Reserved, and rapp-moment/1.0 is PolyForm-Noncommercial 1.0.0 + trademarked marks ('RAPP', 'Holographic Moment(s)'). A consumer/redistributor must check per-repo terms, not assume MIT.
- SECURITY GAP: the Leviathan fleet protocol's POST /api/agent/<name> route is UNAUTHENTICATED — a shell-capable agent on any body = fleet-wide RCE from one request. It is explicitly LAN/trusted-subnet only; there is no auth layer in the spec. Also the canonical fleet wire is now rapp-fleet-chat/1.0 (signed twin-chat over /chat); the legacy /api/agent route is the Phase-1 RCE.

*Generated from `registry.json` (2026-07-11).*
